Petition For Order Directing Transfer And Conveyance Of Trust Property
Off Calendar Mar-07-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Boessenecker for John Garrett and for Paula Bibbs (Petitioners). Jack Valinoti for Pamela Williams and for Chester Williams (Respondents).

Mr. Boessenecker stated that he filed a Notice of Change of Successor Trustee on January 26, 2011, that Ms. Shaw resigned and that Paula Bibbs took over. In addition, Mr. Boessenecker stated that they did not go to mediation, that he was satisfied with the accounting, that the cash that was taken was returned, and that they still have issues with the Attorney's fees and damages. The Accounting Petition, the Petition for Order Directing Transfer and Conveyance of Trust Property, and the issues of Attorney's fees and damages were referred to the Department of the Presiding Judge (206) for trial setting (order for the same signed in open Court).

Judge: John Dearman. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

Petition For Order Directing Transfer And Conveyance Of Trust Property
Off Calendar Nov-16-2011 Continued To Feb-01-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Boessenecker for Shirley Shaw. Jack Valinoti for Pamela Williams and Chester Williams (Respondents).

Mr. Valinoti stated that he hoped to have the accounting done within 30-days. Status of the accounting was set for December 21, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than February 1, 2012. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by January 26, 2012. The Petition for Order Directing Transfer and Conveyance of Trust Property was continued to February 1, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and the Parties are to return on that date for status of mediation.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy A. Whitehead.

Petition For Order Directing Transfer And Conveyance Of Trust Property
Off Calendar Sep-14-2011 Continued To Nov-16-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Directing Transfer And Conveyance Of Trust Property
Off Calendar Aug-10-2011 Continued To Sep-14-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Directing Transfer And Conveyance Of Trust Property
Off Calendar Jun-29-2011 Continued To Aug-10-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Directing Transfer And Conveyance Of Trust Property
Off Calendar Apr-06-2011 Continued To Jun-29-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Joseph Urbansky for Respondent. John Boessenecker for Petitioner.

Mr. Boessencker stated that the reason for continuances was because they were attempting to reconstruct Ms. Garrett's financial situation, that he obtained some financial records, asked the Court to order an accounting from and after the date of death (July 2004) of Mr. Garrett and raised the issue of a $200,000.00 cash withdrawal made in 2004. The Court ordered Respondents to file a petition for approval of the accounting in Court approved format. Mr. Boessencecker will provide Mr. Urbansky with the documents he has obtained. The matter was continued to June 29, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for trailing of the remaining issues in the Petition for Order Directing Transfer and Conveyance of Trust Property and status of filing of the accounting petition.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition For Order Directing Transfer And Conveyance Of Trust Property
Off Calendar Mar-09-2011 Continued To Apr-06-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Off Calendar Oct-20-2010 Continued To Nov-17-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Directing Transfer And Conveyance Of Trust Property
Off Calendar Sep-08-2010 Continued To Oct-20-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Directing Transfer And Conveyance Of Trust Property
Off Calendar Jul-13-2010 Continued To Sep-08-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Joseph Urbanski for Pamela Williams and Chester Williams (Respondents). John Boessenecker for Shirley Shaw.

Mr. Urbanski stated that he was in the process of gathering documents so that he can prepare a formal accounting, that there was some question with regard to the requested date of January 1, 2004 for the accounting, that the Trust was set up in 2006 and that he did not know if Ms. Williams had any receipts. Mr. Boessenecker stated that Ms. Williams was the Attorney in Fact for Ms. Garrett at the time of the Husband's passing and that he believes they have a copy of the Power of Attorney. Counsel agreed that things are on hold pending the accounting. The matter was continued to September 8, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Santiago.

Petition For Order Directing Transfer And Conveyance Of Trust Property
Off Calendar May-25-2010 Continued To Jul-13-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Joseph Urbanski for Pamela Williams and Chester Williams. John Boessenecker for Shirley Shaw. Sadalia Garrett Reel, Cynthia Garrett-Harris and Leonard Garrett (Family Members) also present.

The Court returned the letter received from Ms. Garrett-Harris and explained that it had not read the letter because it would be considered ex parte communication as it had not been circulated to all Parties. Mr. Urbanski stated that he filed the informal accounting for the purpose of providing information to opposing Counsel and that the assets of the Trust were being turned over to the Successor Trustee. Mr. Boessenecker stated that he needed time to review the accounting. The matter was continued to July 13, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Garrett-Harris expressed her concerns about elder financial abuse and stated that she wanted to be sure that all of the funds are brought to light.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

Petition For Order Directing Transfer And Conveyance Of Trust Property
Off Calendar May-04-2010 Continued To May-25-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Joseph Urbanski for Pamela Williams and Chester Williams.

Mr. Urbanski informed the Court that opposing Counsel John Boessenecker was in Court on another matter in Department 301, requested a two week continuance, stated that he would file the account either today or tomorrow and that he received the account number for the investments funds. The matter was continued to May 25, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

Petition For Order Directing Transfer And Conveyance Of Trust Property
Off Calendar Mar-23-2010 Continued To May-04-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Joseph Urbanski for Pamela Williams and Chester Williams (Respondents). Elizabeth Larssen for John Garrett (Estate planning Attorney). John Boessenecker for and with Shirley Shaw.

Mr. Boessenecker reported that he received proof that $141,000.00 (liquid assets) were transferred by Ms. Williams to the Attorney Client trust account, that there was an additional amount of about $150,000.00 to $160,000.00 in securities that could not be transferred and stated that there was a question by Respondent as to the neutrality of Ms. Shaw. In addition, he stated that he would file a declaration as to how Ms. Larssen sought Ms. Shaw and with regard to the neutrality issue, that Pamela would file an accounting and that she would turn over the personal and financial records of Ms. Garrett. Furthermore, he stated that they would get together to discuss how much time Pamela would need to file the accounting, the funds in the trust account, the transfer of those funds, that they would try to resolve the issues one at a time and that they would notify the Examiner if they hit a road block. The matter was continued to May 4, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

Petition For Order Directing Transfer And Conveyance Of Trust Property
Off Calendar Mar-09-2010 Continued To Mar-23-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Joseph Urbanski for Pamela Williams and Chester Williams (Respondents). Elizabeth Larssen for John Laverne Garrett (Estate Planner). John Boessenecker for and with Shirley Shaw and for John Laverne Garrett.

The Court asked if the Parties were willing to participate in mediation. Mr. Boessenecker stated that Ms. Garrett wanted her money back, requested that $300,000.00 be put into an Attorney Client trust account, that it would show good faith and that they could work on the other issues. Mr Urbanski stated that his Clients paid the utilities and the property taxes, and that they agreed to continue to pay these bills. The matter was continued to March 23, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of the funds and to see whether the matter should go to mediation.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.
